frontend_frameworks: React - Component-based UI library using JSX and virtual DOM for efficient updates, steep learning curve but powerful
Vue - Progressive framework balancing simplicity and power, gentle learning curve with single-file components
Angular - Full-featured framework with strong typing and dependency injection, complex but enterprise-ready
Svelte - Compiler-based framework shipping less JavaScript, reactive variables without virtual DOM overhead
Next.js - React meta-framework with server-side rendering, static generation, and API routes
backend_frameworks: Django - Python framework with batteries included, ORM, admin panel, ideal for rapid development
Flask - Lightweight Python framework, minimal but flexible, good for learning and microservices
Spring Boot - Java framework for scalable applications, auto-configuration, embedded server
Express - Node.js framework, minimal and flexible, perfect for REST APIs and real-time applications
FastAPI - Modern Python framework with automatic documentation, type validation, very fast
database_patterns: ORM (Object-Relational Mapping) - Maps database tables to code objects, reduces SQL boilerplate
Migrations - Version control for database schema, track changes and enable reproducible deployments
Query Optimization - Use indexes, join strategies, avoid N+1 queries for database performance
Connection Pooling - Reuse database connections for efficiency, reduces overhead of opening/closing
Data Normalization - Organize data to reduce duplication and maintain consistency
api_patterns: REST - Representational state transfer using HTTP methods on resources, stateless and cacheable
GraphQL - Query language for APIs, fetch exactly what you need, reduces over-fetching
JSON-RPC - Remote procedure call using JSON, simpler than REST for function-like APIs
WebSocket - Two-way communication for real-time applications, persistent connection
gRPC - High-performance RPC using Protocol Buffers, great for internal services
architectural_patterns: MVC (Model-View-Controller) - Separate data, presentation, and logic for maintainability
Microservices - Build applications as small independent services, scalable but complex
Serverless - Functions as a service without managing servers, pay per execution
Event-Driven - Systems react to events through asynchronous communication, loosely coupled
CQRS - Command-Query Responsibility Segregation, separate read and write operations
